title: vitis quinquangularis var. bellula
intro: Vitis quinquangularis var. bellula, also known as the "five-angled grapevine" or "Chinese bell-shaped grapevine", is a plant species within the grape family native to southern China. The plant is said to possess medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and fruit, which have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Vitis quinquangularis var. bellula include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral effects, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting immune function. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as dried and used in capsule or powder form, to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
